nodes.context: French-Canadian outlaw motorcycle gang based in Sherbrooke, Quebec, involved in drug trafficking, robbery, and sexual violence. Active 1967-1984.
International outlaw motorcycle club that expanded into Canada, patching over the Gitans MC in 1984 to form the Sherbrooke charter.
Powerful Québécois biker gang that supplied drugs and weapons to the Gitans MC and later became the first Hells Angels chapter in Canada (Montreal).
Sherbrooke-based outlaw biker gang rival to the Gitans MC, involved in drug trafficking and sexual assault. Dissolved in 1984 after being wiped out by the Gitans.
Sherbrooke street gang in the mid-1960s that evolved into the Dirty Reich MC and later the Gitans MC.
Outlaw motorcycle club that preceded the Gitans MC, retaining the same center-patch logo with Nazi-inspired flags.

edges.context: The Gitans MC patched over to become the Hells Angels Sherbrooke charter in December 1984.
The Popeyes supplied drugs and weapons to the Gitans and maintained positive relations.
The Gitans and Atomes fought a turf war from 1973 to 1975, resulting in six murders, before reaching a truce. The Gitans later wiped out the Atomes in 1984.
The Popeyes became the first Canadian Hells Angels chapter (Montreal) in 1977.
The Vikings street gang evolved into the Dirty Reich MC outlaw motorcycle club.
The Dirty Reich MC rebranded as the Gitans MC in 1970, keeping the same center-patch logo.
